descriptions.docx_handler: Word document (.docx) handler, supports three operations: read, format conversion, and analysis. Conversion supports md/txt/pdf formats (consistent with sidecar word_handler.convert actual support).
descriptions.xlsx_handler: Excel document (.xlsx) handler, supports three operations: read, format conversion, and analysis. Conversion supports csv/pdf/html/txt formats (consistent with sidecar excel_handler.convert actual support).
descriptions.pptx_handler: PowerPoint presentation (.pptx) handler, supports two operations: read and analysis.
descriptions.pdf_handler: PDF document (.pdf) handler, supports three operations: read, format conversion, and analysis. Conversion supports txt/md/html formats (consistent with sidecar pdf_handler.convert actual support).
descriptions.validator_handler: Document quality validator, detects common document quality issues and returns a list of warnings. Supports six types: docx/xlsx/pptx/pdf/md/txt. Markdown detects unclosed code blocks / heading level jumps / trailing whitespace / consecutive blank lines; plain text detects mixed line endings / mixed indentation / overlong lines / consecutive blank lines. Returns {valid, warnings, stats}.

descriptions.list_directory: List files and subdirectories in a specified directory. Use cases: browse workspace contents, locate files, understand directory hierarchy. Supports depth control and extension filtering.
descriptions.search_files: Search files in a specified directory, supporting search by filename or content. Use cases: find files by name, search by content keywords, filter by extension. Set include_content=true to search file contents.
descriptions.read_file: Read plain text file contents (.txt/.md/.csv/.json/.xml, etc.), does not depend on Sidecar, faster. Note: only for plain text files; for reading structured documents like Word/Excel/PPT/PDF, use the read operation of docx_handler/xlsx_handler/pptx_handler/pdf_handler. File size limit 1MB.
descriptions.file_info: Get file metadata (size, modification time, type, etc.). Use cases: understand file information before reading, check file type, confirm file exists and is accessible. Prefer this tool when file contents are not needed.
descriptions.file_exists: Check whether a file or directory exists. Use cases: verify paths before reading or modifying files, avoid operating on non-existent files. Lighter than list_directory.
descriptions.delete_file: Delete a specified file, optionally creating a backup before deletion. Note: this operation is irreversible and will automatically trigger user confirmation. Recommend creating a version snapshot before deletion.
descriptions.create_directory: Create a directory (supports recursive creation). Use cases: ensure target directory exists before writing files, organize file structure. Creates parent directories recursively by default.
descriptions.write_text_file: Write plain text file contents (.txt/.md/.csv/.json, etc.), does not depend on Sidecar. Use cases: create plain text files, modify Markdown files, save JSON configurations. Supports append mode. Note: only for plain text; for generating structured documents, use the generate operation of docx_handler/xlsx_handler/pptx_handler/pdf_handler. Content size limit 4KB (about 4000 characters); exceeding this may trigger LLM response truncation.
descriptions.rename_file: Rename or move a file. Use cases: organize file structure, modify filenames. Note: cross-filesystem moves may fail; this operation is irreversible.
descriptions.copy_file: Copy a file to a new path. Use cases: create file copies, backup files, copy templates. Supports binary file copying.
descriptions.delete_directory: Recursively delete a directory and all its contents. Note: this operation is irreversible and will automatically trigger user confirmation. Recommend confirming directory contents before deletion.
descriptions.get_file_hash: Compute the SHA-256 hash value of a file. Use cases: file deduplication, integrity verification, change detection. Returns a hexadecimal hash string.
descriptions.read_file_lines: Read a plain text file line by line, supporting offset and line count limits. Use cases: read specific portions of large files, paginate reads, view the tail of log files. Recommended for paginated reading of large files.
descriptions.update_notes: Agent scratchpad: record or read task notes to maintain context across iteration rounds. Use cases: record key decisions, to-do items, file paths, and intermediate results in complex multi-step tasks. Recommend calling action=add to record key points after completing critical steps; when task context grows long, action=read can review existing notes; after task completion, action=clear cleans up. Note contents are automatically injected into your context in subsequent iterations, no need to read repeatedly.

builtin-weekly-report.name: Weekly Report
builtin-weekly-report.description: Automatically generate a structured weekly report document from this week's work
builtin-weekly-report.content:
Please generate a professional weekly report Word document (.docx) with clear structure and traceable data.

## Content Structure
1. Summary Table — task name, priority (High/Mid/Low), status (Done/In Progress/Not Started), completion %, remarks
2. Key Progress — highlight 2-3 core milestone achievements, use data where applicable
3. Issues Encountered — table: issue description, impact scope, severity, current status (Resolved/In Progress/Pending)
4. Risks & Mitigation — identify key risks, mitigation measures, owners
5. Next Week Plan — prioritized detailed plan with deliverables and deadlines

## Document Format
- Title: Arial 18pt bold centered; H1: Arial 14pt bold; H2: Arial 12pt bold
- Body: Times New Roman 10.5pt, 1.25 line spacing
- Tables: styled with bold header row, light gray fill
- Margins: 1 inch all sides (Word default)
- Page size: Letter

## Output
- Save to workspace, filename format: Weekly_Report_YYYY-MM-DD.docx
- Run validator_handler to verify document quality after generation

Work content:
{{content}}

builtin-meeting-minutes.name: Meeting Minutes
builtin-meeting-minutes.description: Generate a standardized meeting minutes document from meeting information
builtin-meeting-minutes.content:
Please generate a professional meeting minutes Word document (.docx) with complete information, traceable decisions, and trackable action items.

## Content Structure
1. Basic Info — meeting topic, time, location, chair, minutes taker, attendees, absentees
2. Agenda — list of topics covered
3. Discussion — per topic: topic name, discussion points,各方观点, conclusions
4. Resolutions — list all resolutions (ID, content, voting result)
5. Action Items Table — ID, description, owner, priority (High/Mid/Low), due date, status
6. Next Meeting — time, location, proposed topics (if any)

## Document Format
- Title: Arial 18pt bold centered; H1: Arial 14pt bold; H2: Arial 12pt bold
- Body: Times New Roman 10.5pt, 1.25 line spacing
- Action items table should include auto-numbering column
- Margins: Word default standard

## Output
- Save to workspace, filename format: Meeting_Minutes_YYYY-MM-DD_Topic.docx
- Run validator_handler to verify document quality after generation

Meeting information:
{{content}}

builtin-data-analysis.name: Data Analysis
builtin-data-analysis.description: Perform statistical analysis on data and generate an analysis report
builtin-data-analysis.content:
Please perform a professional statistical analysis on the specified data file and generate a complete data analysis report.

## Analysis Workflow
1. Data Overview — rows, columns, data types, missing values, descriptive statistics
2. Data Cleaning — document any missing value handling, outlier treatment, transformations
3. Key Metrics — compute core statistics (mean, median, std dev, quartiles, growth rates) based on analysis focus
4. Trend Analysis — identify trends, cycles, seasonality; use appropriate charts
5. Comparative Analysis — group comparisons, before/after, or benchmark comparisons
6. Anomaly Detection — flag outliers, analyze potential causes and impact
7. Conclusions & Recommendations — actionable insights based on data

## Document Format
- Cover page (title, date, analyst), table of contents, body, appendix
- Charts must have figure numbers and captions (e.g., Figure 1: Quarterly Sales Trend)
- Font: Heading bold, body 10.5pt, 1.25 line spacing
- Reference statistical methods in appendix

## Output
- Save as Word document to workspace
- Filename format: Data_Analysis_Report_YYYY-MM-DD.docx
- Use matplotlib for visualizations, embed charts in docx
- Run validator_handler to verify quality

File path: {{filePath}}
Analysis focus: {{focus}}

builtin-format-convert.name: Format Conversion
builtin-format-convert.description: Convert a document from one format to another
builtin-format-convert.content:
Please convert the specified file from source format to target format, ensuring conversion quality and content integrity.

## Conversion Workflow
1. Source Inspection — read source file to confirm format and content integrity
2. Execute Conversion — use appropriate document processing tool
3. Post-Validation — read target file to verify content completeness and format correctness
4. Limitations Note — document any features not supported by target format (e.g., Excel formulas to PDF)

## Quality Requirements
- Text: fully preserved, no garbled characters or loss
- Tables: row/column structure and merged cells preserved
- Images: preserved where possible, note position changes
- Styles: formatting preserved as much as possible (font, size, color, alignment)

## Output
- Provide conversion summary (source size, target size, duration, notes)

Source file: {{inputPath}}
Source format: {{sourceFormat}}
Target format: {{targetFormat}}
Output path: {{outputPath}}

builtin-doc-review.name: Document Review
builtin-doc-review.description: Review document content and suggest modifications
builtin-doc-review.content:
Please perform a professional review of the specified document, evaluating quality across multiple dimensions with specific recommendations.

## Review Dimensions & Scoring
Rate each dimension (1-5) with comments:
1. Accuracy — facts, data, citations correct?
2. Logic — clear structure, evidence supporting claims, sound reasoning
3. Completeness — all required elements present, no significant gaps
4. Standards Compliance — format, terminology, citation standards met
5. Readability — clear and concise language suitable for target audience

## Issue Format
For each issue found, report:
- Location: page/section
- Type: Accuracy/Logic/Completeness/Standards/Readability
- Severity: Critical/Major/Minor
- Description: specific problem identified
- Suggestion: actionable fix recommendation

## Summary
Provide overall assessment: total score, key strengths, priority improvement areas, critical issues list

File path: {{filePath}}
Review focus: {{focus}}

builtin-ppt-outline.name: PPT Outline Generation
builtin-ppt-outline.description: Generate a PPT outline and content based on a topic
builtin-ppt-outline.content:
Please generate a professional PowerPoint presentation (.pptx) based on the topic below, with substantial content, professional visuals, suitable for formal presentation.

## Content Structure
1. Title slide — title, subtitle (optional), date, presenter
2. Table of Contents — list of sections
3. Content slides — each with title, 3-5 bullet points, optional diagrams or tables
4. Section divider slides — before each major section
5. Summary slide — key takeaways, next steps
6. Appendix slides (optional) — supporting data, references

## Design Guidelines
- Consistent color scheme and font style throughout
- Title font size >= 28pt, body font size >= 18pt
- Keep slides uncluttered, follow "less is more" principle
- Use charts for data rather than text tables
- Add speaker notes for key slides

## Output
- Save as PPTX to workspace
- Filename format: Presentation_Topic.pptx
- Run validator_handler to verify quality

Topic: {{topic}}
Target slide count: about {{pageCount}} pages

builtin-tech-proposal.name: Technical Proposal
builtin-tech-proposal.description: Generate a professional technical proposal document
builtin-tech-proposal.content:
Please generate a complete technical proposal Word document (.docx) for project review and technical decision-making.

## Content Structure
1. Cover page — project name, document version, date, classification
2. Revision history — table: version, date, changes, author
3. Glossary — terms and abbreviations used
4. Project Overview — background, objectives, constraints
5. Requirements Analysis — functional summary, non-functional requirements (performance, security, availability)
6. Architecture Design — architecture description, technology choices with rationale, module breakdown
7. Detailed Design — core design points, interface definitions, data model
8. Implementation Plan — phases, milestones, resource needs, deliverables
9. Risk Assessment — technical risks, mitigation strategies

## Document Format
- Title: 18pt bold centered; H1: 14pt bold; H2: 12pt bold
- Body: 10.5pt, 1.25 line spacing
- Code blocks in monospace font with gray background
- Header: project name centered; Footer: page number centered

## Output
- Save to workspace, filename format: Technical_Proposal_ProjectName_YYYY-MM-DD.docx
- Run validator_handler to verify quality

Project name: {{projectName}}
Background: {{background}}
Core requirements: {{requirements}}

builtin-business-letter.name: Business Letter
builtin-business-letter.description: Generate a formal business correspondence document
builtin-business-letter.content:
Please generate a formal business letter Word document (.docx) with proper formatting and appropriate language.

## Content Structure
1. Letterhead — organization name, logo placeholder, address, contact info
2. Title — Regarding: [Subject], centered bold
3. Reference number — organization acronym [Year] No.X, right-aligned
4. Recipient — full organization name, flush left
5. Body — opening (context), main content (structured points), closing (thanks or reply request)
6. Signature — organization name, date, contact person
7. Attachment note (if any) — name and quantity

## Style Requirements
- Formal, concise, accurate language
- Clear paragraphs, one topic per paragraph
- Cite sources for data or references
- Avoid vague expressions; specify time, quantities clearly

## Document Format
- Title: 16pt bold centered
- Body: 12pt, 1.5 line spacing
- Margins: proper business letter standard
- Page number: bottom center

## Output
- Save to workspace, filename format: Letter_Subject_YYYY-MM-DD.docx
- Run validator_handler to verify quality

Recipient: {{recipient}}
Purpose: {{purpose}}
Details: {{details}}

builtin-project-plan.name: Project Plan
builtin-project-plan.content:
Please generate a complete project plan Word document (.docx) for project initiation and execution tracking.

## Content Structure
1. Cover — project name, version, date, authoring organization
2. Revision history — version, date, changes, author
3. Project Overview — background, SMART objectives, scope, key stakeholders
4. WBS — work breakdown by phase/module using multi-level lists
5. Milestone Plan — table: milestone name, deliverable, acceptance criteria, target date
6. Detailed Schedule — table: task ID, name, dependencies, owner, start/end dates, duration
7. Resource Plan — human resources, equipment/tools, budget estimate
8. Quality Management — quality standards, review mechanism, acceptance process
9. Risk Management — risk register: description, probability, impact, strategy, owner
10. Communication Plan — audience, method, frequency, content

## Document Format
- Title: 18pt bold centered; H1: 14pt bold; H2: 12pt bold
- Body: 10.5pt, 1.25 line spacing
- Professional styled tables with color-coded key rows/columns
- Header: project name; Footer: page number

## Output
- Save to workspace, filename format: Project_Plan_ProjectName_YYYY-MM-DD.docx
- Run validator_handler to verify quality

Project name: {{projectName}}
Objectives: {{objectives}}
Timeline: {{timeline}}

shortcuts.sendMessageDesc: Send the message in the input box

help.configureLLMDesc: Add OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini API configuration in Settings
help.selectWorkspaceDesc: Specify the directory for documents, Agent will operate files in this directory
help.startConversationDesc: Describe your needs in the input box, Agent will automatically select the appropriate Handler to complete the task
help.handlerValidator: Document quality validator (detects common document quality issues)
help.faqA0: In Settings > LLM Configuration, click the "Add Provider" button, select the provider type (OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini/Ollama/Custom), and fill in the API URL, API Key, and model name.
help.faqA1: Supports Word(.docx), Excel(.xlsx), PowerPoint(.pptx), PDF(.pdf) and Markdown(.md) format generation, reading and modification, as well as mutual conversion between these formats.
help.faqQ2: How to create a custom Handler?
help.faqA2: In Settings > Handlers Management, click the "Add Custom Handler" button. Custom Handlers are essentially Prompt templates that support {{param_name}} placeholders, which are automatically replaced when Agent calls them.
help.faqQ3: How does Agent confirm high-risk operations?
help.faqA3: When Agent performs high-risk operations such as deletion or overwriting, a confirmation dialog will pop up, and you can choose to approve or reject. The confirmation level can be adjusted in Settings > General (Always confirm / Confirm edits only / Auto-confirm all).
help.faqQ4: How to view document version history?
help.faqA4: Right-click a file in the file tree and select "Version History", or click the version history button in the document preview panel. A version snapshot is automatically created before each document modification, and you can roll back to any historical version.
help.faqA5: Click the workspace selector in the top bar to switch workspaces, or add, switch and manage workspaces in Settings > Workspace Management. Each workspace's files and configuration are independent.
help.faqA6: You can create and manage commonly used Prompt templates in Settings > Prompt Templates, with support for variable placeholders. You can quickly insert template content through the template picker in the input box.

preview.emptyPage: No text content on this page
preview.autoSnapshotHint: Version snapshots are automatically created when documents are modified
